FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE - SCHEDULE OF FEES AND CHARGES. LOAN 1207163587 FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE 02/23/2024 Schedule of Fees and Charges. This Schedule of Fees and Charges is furnished by Fairway Independent Mortgage ("Lender," "we," "us," or "our") to Lawrence Dixon ("Borrower," "you," or "your") in connection with the loan identified as 1207163587 and secured by the property located at 39985 Leslie Crescent, Alexandraburough, OK 04324. It describes the categories of fees, service charges, and reimbursable advances that may be assessed in the servicing of your account under applicable law and the terms of your note and security instrument. This Schedule is provided for informational purposes, does not modify the loan documents, and should be read together with them. Fees described here may not apply to your transaction, and additional charges permitted by law may be assessed as circumstances require. 1. GENERAL FEE TERMS. The fees and charges summarized in this Schedule are imposed in accordance with the terms of your note, your security instrument, and applicable federal and OK law. Not every fee described will apply to your loan; whether a particular charge is assessed depends on the activity concerning your account, the services you request, and what items you may provide; it is not necessarily a standard charge. For example, charges may vary by provider, but efforts are made to ensure they are reasonable and customary in the market. In all cases where the fee is not specified in the Schedule and is not dictated by law, the result may be manipulated to ensure compliance. Unless otherwise directed, these fees are considered to be standard and subject to the guidelines outlined herein. The list of charges that you may incur may be extensive and is not exhaustive; there are other charges which may apply to your loan that may not appear on this Schedule. To find out if you will be charged on your specific instrument, or applicable law, the controlling document or the law governing your note, you must specifically request information upon request, subject to any charge for documents disclosed elsewhere in this Schedule.

1. RETURNED PAYMENT CHARGES If any payment, instrument, or other tender submitted toward your account is returned, dishonored, reversed, or otherwise not honored by the financial institution on which it is drawn, for any reason, we may assess a returned-item charge in the maximum amount permitted by applicable law and by your loan documents. This charge applies regardless of the form of the tender, including checks, drafts, electronic funds transfers, and automated clearing house entries, and regardless of whether the dishonor results from insufficient funds, a closed or frozen account, a stop-payment order, or other cause. A returned payment does not constitute payment of the installment to which it was directed, and the related installment will remain due and unpaid until satisfied by good funds. Consequently, a returned payment may also result in the assessment of a late charge subject to the limitations provided in the applicable grace period, and may affect the reporting of your account to credit bureaus. Following a returned item, we may, to the extent permitted by law, require that future payments be made by certified funds, cashier's check, money order, or another guaranteed method for a reasonable period. The returned-item charge is intended to compensate us for the administrative costs and processing expense associated with handling a dishonored tender and is in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other remedy available to us. Where applicable, we may establish a maximum dollar amount for this charge and the frequency with which this charge may be imposed, will observe this limit. Returned-item charges, once assessed, are due and payable and may be added to the balance of your account if not paid separately. 2. LATE CHARGES Your note establishes the date on which each periodic payment is due and the grace period, if any, within which payment may be received without a late charge. If the full amount of a scheduled payment is not received by the end of the applicable grace period, we may assess a late charge on the delinquent amount. The late charge is contingent upon the date the payment has already occurred. Partial payments do not relieve the obligation to pay the full scheduled amount unless previously agreed to in writing. The terms of any repayment will be elaborated upon and explained on specific payment due dates you receive. A late charge is assessed when a payment is received after the due date, and is not affected by the subsequent payment of the amount not previously received. Your subsequent payment does not relieve the obligation to pay the late charge as it may apply to the earlier non-payment. Late charges are due and payable when assessed and may be collected from delinquent payments to the extent permitted by law. The amount assessed will depend upon the specific delinquency, and does not establish a waiver of the right to assess late charges on other delinquent installments.

4. PAYOFF AND DEMAND STATEMENT FEES Upon request, we will prepare and furnish a statement setting forth the amount required to pay your loan in full as a specified date, commonly referred to as a payoff statement, demand statement, or beneficiary statement. Where permitted by applicable OK law and your loan documents, we may charge a reasonable fee for the preparation and delivery of such a statement, including any required delivery by facsimile, electronic mail, or other expedited means. Certain jurisdictions require that a payoff statement be provided without charge or within a prescribed period, and we will comply with any such requirement. A payoff statement reflects amounts due as of the stated good-through date and is contingent upon the receipt of used funds on or before that date. Because interest, fees, advances, and other charges may continue to accrue, a payoff statement may include a per-diem interest figure and may expire if payment is not received by the good-through date, in which event an updated statement may be needed and an additional preparation fee may apply. Amounts shown in a payoff statement remain subject to final verification, including the clearance of recent payments and the resolution of any pending charges. We may also charge reasonable fees, to the extent permitted by law, for the preparation and recording of documents necessary to release, recover, or satisfy the lien securing your loan upon payment in full, as well as for any escrow, title, or governmental charges that may be associated with such release. Fees associated with lien release or other releases are typically disclosed at or before payoff and may be required to be collected as part of the payoff amount in accordance with applicable OK law. 5. OPTIONAL EXPEDITED PAYMENT SERVICES As a convenience, we may from time to time offer optional services that allow you to make a payment by telephone, through an online portal, or through another customer service representative, with expedited processing. These services are entirely optional. You are never required to use an expedited payment service to make a timely payment, and you may always submit your payment without charge by mail and using the standard payment methods provided to you. If you choose to use an expedited payment service for which a fee is charged, the fee, the available processing options, and the cutoff times for same-day processing will be disclosed to you before you make your payment, along with instructions to proceed consistent with the applicable fee. Such convenience fees compensate for the cost of processing your payment. We will impose any such fees in accordance with applicable law. If you use an expedited payment service, your obligations remain the same as they would be for any other payment, and you still must ensure that you make your payment on or before the applicable due date to avoid any additional charges. Using an expedited payment method does not alter your obligation to make your payment on time, and any such payment will be credited to your account as of the date the payment is actually received in good funds.

6. PROPERTY INSPECTION AND PRESERVATION
If your loan becomes delinquent, or the property securing your loan appears to be vacant, abandoned, or inadequately maintained, you may, to the extent permitted by your security instrument and applicable OK law, conduct or arrange property inspections to assess the condition and occupancy of the property. The reasonable cost of each such inspection may be charged to your account, inspections performed to protect the value of the property and our interest in it and to not relieve you of your obligation to maintain the property as required by your loan documents.

While the property is found to require protective measures, we may incur expenses to preserve and protect it, which may include securing the premises, winterization, debris removal, repair of conditions that threaten habitability or value, and similar property-preservation activities authorized by your security instrument. The costs for such preservation efforts may be advanced by us and charged to your account as amounts secured by the security instrument, bearing interest as and to the extent provided in your loan documents.

Property Inspection and preservation charges reflect amounts billed to us by third-party vendors or governmental authorities and may vary with the location, condition, and circumstances of the property. We will assess such charges only as permitted by applicable law and your loan documents, and we will not charge for an inspection or preservation activity more frequently than is reasonably necessary to protect the security for the loan.

7. FORCE-PLACED INSURANCE CHARGES
Your security instrument requires you to maintain hazard, flood, and any other insurance coverage required by the property securing your loan. If you fail to maintain required coverage, or if you do not provide satisfactory evidence of such coverage upon request, we may, after providing any notices required by applicable law, purchase insurance to protect our interest in the property. This insurance may cover you, but will most likely cover only us, as the named insured beneficiary. We strongly encourage you to maintain your own insurance at all times to cover your interest in the property. If we arrange force-placed insurance, we will apply any coverage proceeds toward the payment of your loan balance, including any unapplied force-placed insurance charges.

Force-placed insurance charges reflect amounts charged to us by the insurer providing the force-placed insurance, and such amounts may vary depending on factors such as the location of the property, replacement costs, and any minimum coverage required by the insurer. We will assess such charges only as permitted by applicable law, unless you obtain proof of insurance coverage on your own. We will provide you with notice of any force-placed insurance assessment and the charges required under this provision which may result in additional costs to you. We will comply with all applicable limitations on the imposition of such charges.

8. TAX-RELATED ADVANCES Your loan documents obligate you to pay, when due, all real property taxes, special assessments, and similar governmental charges levied against the property securing your loan. If such taxes or assessments become delinquent and are not paid, or if a tax lien or sheriff's, or similar interest in the property, we may, as permitted by your security instrument and applicable OK law, advance funds to pay the delinquent taxes, assessments, penalties, and related charges in order to protect the security for the loan. Amounts so advanced may be charged to your account. Tax-related advances reflect amounts paid by us to taxing authorities and may include penalties, interest, and redemption costs imposed by those authorities, as well as reasonable service charges for the research and processing necessary to identify and satisfy the obligation. Such advances are treated as amounts secured by the security instrument and may bear interest as to the extent provided in your loan documents. Making a tax advance does not waive any default arising from your failure to pay taxes when due, nor does it obligate us to make any further advance. If your loan includes an escrow or impound account for the payment of taxes and insurance, the administration of that account, including any shortage, surplus, or deficiency, is governed by the separate escrow disclosures provided to you by applicable, including the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act. Tax-related advances described in this section generally arise when there is no escrow account, when escrowed funds are insufficient, or where amounts otherwise remain unpaid and threaten the priority or security of our lien. 9. DOCUMENT AND RESEARCH FEES Upon your request, and to the extent permitted by applicable OK and federal law and your loan documents, we may provide copies of loan documents, account histories, year-end statements, amortization schedules, and similar records, and we may also charge a fee for research, including a fee for unusual or complex matters. Certain documents and disclosures are also required by law, and you may be charged a fee for any documents that are requested in excess of those amounts provided for by your loan documents. We will also charge a fee for processing documents where your document request is a second or additional request. We may also charge our reasonable fees for special services you request that fall outside the ordinary scope of our usual operations to provide you with servicing of your loan. In all cases, the fees charged will not exceed the amounts permitted by applicable law, and we will notify you in writing of those fees before providing any documents. You may decline to accept any charges applicable to your request for documents and disclosures. We may also charge a fee for processing documents provided to any third parties, such as government agencies, not primarily under the control of us, where additional time and expense are incurred. In any such instances, we will notify you of the costs involved for your acceptance. Where a required notice is to be given to a governmental authority or other third party, such as a tax assessor, real estate office, or any other agency, you may decline our offer to provide such notice or processing or research fee, provided you are offered the service so that you may decide whether to proceed.

10. DEFAULT AND COLLECTION COSTS In the event of a default under your note or security instrument, you may, to the extent permitted by those documents and applicable OK and federal law, be responsible for the reasonable costs and expenses we incur in enforcing our rights and protecting our interest in the property. Such costs may include, without limitation, attorneys' fees, court costs, trustee's fees, and lien search charges, foreclosure and bankruptcy-related fees, and other expenses authorized by your loan documents and permitted by law. These amounts may be added to the balance of your account, as provided in your security instrument.

Default-related charges reflect amounts billed to us by attorneys, trustees, courts, and other third parties, and the nature and amount of such charges depend on the stage of any enforcement proceeding, the jurisdiction in which the property is located, and the requirements of applicable law. We will assess such charges only to the extent they are actually incurred, are reasonable, and are permitted by your loan documents and applicable law, and we will observe any statutory limitations, notice requirements, and reinstatement rights that apply.

Curing a default by bringing your account current, when a right to do so exists, may require payment of accrued default and collection costs in addition to the past-due amounts, to the extent permitted by law and your loan documents. Nothing in this section creates an obligation on our part to accept a partial cure or to forebear from exercising any remedy, except as required by applicable law or by the terms of your loan documents.

11. HOW FEES MAY CHANGE The fees and charges described in this Schedule may change from time to time. Many of the amounts reflect charges imposed on us by third parties or by governmental authorities, which we do not control and which may increase or decrease without notice to you. Other Fees may reflect changes in our internal policies or procedures, or changes in the maximum amounts permitted by your loan documents and applicable OK and federal law. There are limits on the amounts we may charge you for certain types of services.

We will provide advance notice to you when notice is required by applicable law or by your loan documents, or otherwise as we determine is appropriate for the services being provided. We may also change whether fees are charged, when they are charged, and the way they are charged. This Schedule has been designed to comply with applicable laws.

In this Schedule we describe your charges and partially assess our discretion not to assess a fee in any situation. We will notify you when we change our charges for any reason, not upon each occasion. If you have questions, you may call us at 877-397-5617 or write in writing at 35385 Lisa Station, Port Tarasade, MS 03372, and we will respond as required by applicable law.

12. WAIVERS AND DISCRETION  
We may, in our sole discretion and without obligation, reduce or waive a particular fee or charge in an individual case, including as an accommodation in connection with a hardship, a payment arrangement, or a loss-mitigation effort. Any such reduction or waiver applies only to the specific charge addressed and only for the occasion on which it is granted. A waiver of one fee is not a waiver of any other fee, is not a modification of your loan documents or this Schedule, and does not establish a pattern, practice, or course of dealing upon which you may rely with respect to future charges.  

No waiver of any law, charge, right, or remedy is effective unless and only to the extent we grant it, and our acceptance of a late or partial payment, or forbearance in collecting a charge, or our delay in exercising a right does not constitute a waiver of that charge or right or of our ability to enforce the full terms of your loan documents. To the extent applicable law requires that a waiver be in writing or follow a particular procedure, we will observe that requirement.  

If you believe you have been assessed in error, or if you wish to request the waiver or review of a charge, you may contact us at 679-307-5161 or submit your request in writing to 53368 Lila Station, Port Tarlac, MS 03372, referencing loan 1207158367. We will review timely and properly directed inquiries and will respond within the period and in the manner required by applicable law, correcting any error and refunding or crediting any amount as required.  

ACKNOWLEDGED BY BORROWER  
Lawrence Dixon

Binding Arbitration Agreement
LOAN 1207158367 / FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE / 02/22/2024
This Binding Arbitration Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between Lawrence Dixon
("you" or "Borrower") and Fairway Independent Mortgage ("we," "us," or "Lender") in connection
with the loan identified as loan number 1207158367 and any account, transaction, or
relationship arising therefrom. By signing the related loan documents or otherwise accepting the
terms set forth herein, you acknowledge that you have read this Agreement, that you
understand it materially affects your legal rights, and that you agree to resolve Covered Disputes
through individual binding arbitration rather than in court before a judge or jury. Please read this
Agreement carefully and retain a copy for your records. Capitalized terms not defined herein
have the meanings given in the loan documents.
1. AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE
You and Fairway Independent Mortgage agree that, except as otherwise expressly provided in this
Agreement, any Covered Dispute (as defined below) shall be resolved by neutral, binding arbitration
administered on an individual basis, and not by a court, jury, or any form of class, collective,
representative, or consolidated proceeding. This Agreement reflects the mutual intention of the parties to
provide a fair, efficient, and confidential forum for the resolution of disputes. By agreeing to arbitrate,
both you and the Lender knowingly and voluntarily relinquish certain rights that would otherwise be
available in a judicial forum, as further described in the sections that follow.
This Agreement is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. Section 1 et seq. (the "FAA"), and
evidences a transaction involving interstate commerce. To the extent the FAA is held inapplicable to any
issue, the arbitration laws of the OK in which the property securing the loan, located at 33968 Leslie
Crescent, Alexandraborough, OK 04324, is situated shall apply, provided that such laws do not conflict
with the FAA. The parties intend that this Agreement be interpreted broadly to give effect to their
agreement to arbitrate the widest range of disputes permitted by applicable law.
This Agreement applies regardless of whether the Covered Dispute arises before, during, or after the
term of the loan, and regardless of whether the loan or the underlying obligation has been paid, satisfied,
charged off, sold, assigned, or otherwise transferred. Your obligations and the Lender's obligations under
this Agreement continue notwithstanding the closing, servicing transfer, or termination of any account or
relationship between the parties.
2. DISPUTES COVERED
A "Covered Dispute" means any claim, dispute, or controversy of any kind, whether based in contract,
tort, statute, regulation, common law, equity, or otherwise, that arises out of or relates in any way to the
Retain this document with your loan file records.

loan, the loan documents, this Agreement, the property securing the loan, the origination, underwriting,
processing, closing, funding, servicing, collection, or enforcement of the loan, or any communication,
disclosure, advertisement, product, or service associated with the relationship between you and Fairway
Independent Mortgage. The term is to be construed in the broadest manner permitted by law.
Covered Disputes also include, without limitation, claims relating to the validity, enforceability, scope, or
interpretation of this Agreement, except as expressly reserved for judicial determination herein; claims
regarding alleged fraud, misrepresentation, breach of fiduciary duty, or unfair or deceptive acts or
practices; claims under federal or state consumer-protection, lending, privacy, or fair-credit-reporting
statutes; and claims arising from any prior loan, account, or transaction between you and the Lender.
Claims by or against the Lender's affiliates, agents, employees, officers, directors, successors, assigns,
and servicers are likewise included.
The fact that a Covered Dispute may also constitute a claim by or against a third party shall not defeat
the obligation to arbitrate as between you and Fairway Independent Mortgage. If any portion of a dispute
falls within the scope of this Agreement and any portion does not, the parties shall arbitrate the portion
that is covered and may stay the remainder pending the outcome of the arbitration, to the extent
permitted by applicable law and the rules of the administering body.
3. DISPUTES EXCLUDED
Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement does not require arbitration of the following matters: any
action by Fairway Independent Mortgage to foreclose upon, or to obtain possession of, the property
securing the loan through judicial or non-judicial foreclosure, power of sale, deed in lieu, ejectment, or
similar remedy authorized under the loan documents or applicable OK law; the exercise of any self-help
remedy, including set-off; or the filing or enforcement of any lien against the collateral. The Lender's
pursuit of such remedies shall not waive its right to compel arbitration of any other Covered Dispute.
This Agreement also does not preclude either party from seeking provisional or ancillary remedies,
including temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, attachment, receivership, or other interim
relief from a court of competent jurisdiction in aid of arbitration or to preserve the status quo pending
arbitration. Seeking or obtaining such relief shall not constitute a waiver of the right or obligation to
arbitrate, and any merits determination of the underlying Covered Dispute shall remain subject to
arbitration.
Nothing in this Agreement limits your right to file a complaint with, or to provide information to, any
federal, state, or local governmental agency or regulatory authority, including any banking or consumer-
protection agency, and to participate in any investigation or proceeding conducted by such authority.
This Agreement does not preclude such an authority from exercising its enforcement powers, nor does it
require you to arbitrate any claim that applicable law expressly provides may not be subjected to pre-
dispute binding arbitration.
4. SMALL-CLAIMS CARVE-OUT
Either you or Fairway Independent Mortgage may elect to bring and pursue an individual claim in a small-
claims court of competent jurisdiction in lieu of arbitration, provided that the claim qualifies for that court
under its jurisdictional limits and remains in that forum on an individual basis. This carve-out is intended
Retain this document with your loan file records.

to preserve a simple, low-cost venue for the resolution of modest individual disputes without the
procedures associated with formal arbitration or litigation.
If a claim filed in small-claims court is transferred, removed, appealed to, or otherwise proceeds in a
court other than a small-claims court, or if the claim is amended to seek relief or to assert allegations that
exceed the jurisdiction of the small-claims court, then either party may thereafter elect to compel
arbitration of that claim under this Agreement. Any such election shall be honored by the parties, and the
dispute shall proceed in arbitration in accordance with the terms set forth herein.
A claim brought in small-claims court must be asserted only in the claimant's individual capacity and
may not be maintained as, or consolidated with, any class, collective, or representative action. The class-
action waiver and other individual-relief provisions of this Agreement apply with equal force to any matter
pursued under this small-claims carve-out.
5. ADMINISTERING BODY AND RULES
Unless the parties agree in writing to a different forum, a Covered Dispute submitted to arbitration shall
be administered by a nationally recognized, neutral arbitration administrator that maintains established
procedures for the resolution of consumer disputes, in accordance with that administrator's consumer
arbitration rules in effect at the time the arbitration is commenced. The arbitration shall be conducted by
a single neutral arbitrator who is a retired judge or an attorney experienced in consumer-finance or real-
property matters, selected in accordance with the administrator's rules.
To the extent the chosen administrator's rules conflict with this Agreement, the terms of this Agreement
shall control to the extent permitted by the administrator and applicable law. If the designated
administrator is unavailable or unwilling to administer the arbitration consistent with this Agreement, the
parties shall jointly select a comparable neutral administrator, and failing agreement, a court of
competent jurisdiction may appoint the administrator or arbitrator upon the petition of either party, as
authorized by the FAA.
The arbitrator shall have the authority to determine the arbitrability of any Covered Dispute and to resolve
any dispute concerning the interpretation, applicability, or enforceability of this Agreement, except that
the enforceability of the class-action and representative-action waiver set forth in this Agreement shall be
determined exclusively by a court and not by the arbitrator. The arbitrator shall apply the substantive law
that a court would apply and shall honor applicable statutes of limitation and claims of privilege. You may
obtain the administrator's rules and forms by contacting Fairway Independent Mortgage at
679-307-5161 or in writing at 53368 Lisa Station, Port Taraside, MS 03372.
6. EFFECT OF ARBITRATION; JURY-TRIAL WAIVER
BY AGREEING TO ARBITRATE COVERED DISPUTES, YOU AND FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE
EACH KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY, AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVE THE RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY
AND THE RIGHT TO HAVE COVERED DISPUTES DECIDED BY A JUDGE IN A COURT OF LAW.
Arbitration substitutes a private dispute-resolution process for the procedures available in court. The
parties acknowledge that this waiver is a material inducement to entering into the loan transaction and
that each has had the opportunity to consult with legal counsel of its own choosing regarding the
consequences of this waiver.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

In arbitration, the proceedings are generally more streamlined than litigation, discovery may be more
limited, and the rights to pre-trial and appellate review are substantially narrower than those available in
court. The arbitrator's decision is final and binding, subject only to the limited grounds for review
provided under the FAA. Judgment on the award rendered by the arbitrator may be entered in any court
of competent jurisdiction. The parties accept these features of arbitration in exchange for its efficiency,
finality, and reduced cost.
The arbitrator may award any relief, including monetary damages, declaratory relief, injunctive relief, and
statutory remedies, that a court could award to an individual party under applicable law, and only in favor
of the individual party seeking relief and only to the extent necessary to provide relief warranted by that
party's individual claim. The arbitrator shall issue a reasoned written award setting forth the essential
findings and conclusions on which the award is based, in accordance with the administrator's rules.
7. CLASS-ACTION AND REPRESENTATIVE WAIVER
COVERED DISPUTES SHALL BE ARBITRATED ONLY ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS. YOU AND FAIRWAY
INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE EACH WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO ARBITRATE OR LITIGATE ANY COVERED
DISPUTE AS A CLASS ACTION, COLLECTIVE ACTION, PRIVATE ATTORNEY-GENERAL ACTION, OR
OTHER REPRESENTATIVE PROCEEDING, OR TO SERVE AS A CLASS REPRESENTATIVE OR CLASS
MEMBER, OR TO PARTICIPATE AS A MEMBER OF A CLASS OF CLAIMANTS. The arbitrator shall have
no authority to consolidate or join the claims of more than one person and may not preside over any form
of class, collective, or representative proceeding.
The parties agree that this class-action and representative waiver is a fundamental and non-severable
element of their agreement to arbitrate. If a court or arbitrator determines that this waiver is
unenforceable or invalid as to a particular Covered Dispute or claim for relief, then, to that extent, that
particular Covered Dispute or claim shall proceed in a court of competent jurisdiction rather than in
arbitration, but all other Covered Disputes and claims shall remain subject to arbitration on an individual
basis as provided in this Agreement.
No arbitration under this Agreement shall be joined or consolidated with any other arbitration involving a
different borrower or party without the written consent of all parties to all affected arbitrations. The
prohibition on class, collective, and representative proceedings applies regardless of the forum and
survives any determination that another provision of this Agreement is unenforceable.
8. COSTS AND FEES
The party initiating an arbitration shall pay the filing fee required by the administrator, except that, upon
your written request, Fairway Independent Mortgage will advance or reimburse all or a portion of the
filing, administrative, and arbitrator fees attributable to a Covered Dispute that you initiate, to the extent
provided by the administrator's consumer rules or as otherwise required to ensure that the cost of
arbitration is not prohibitive relative to the cost of pursuing the claim in court. Such advancement does
not waive any right to seek recovery of those amounts in the award.
Each party shall otherwise bear its own attorneys' fees, expert fees, and other costs incurred in
connection with the arbitration, except that the arbitrator may award attorneys' fees and costs to the
prevailing party to the extent such an award would be available under the applicable substantive law
Retain this document with your loan file records.

governing the Covered Dispute, or as otherwise provided by the loan documents. The allocation of fees
and costs shall be set forth in the arbitrator's award and is subject to the same finality as the remainder
of the award.

This Privacy Notice describes how Fairway Independent Mortgage ("Fairway Independent Mortgage," "we," "us," or "our") collects, shares, and protects the

otherwise required or permitted by law.
§ 1. WHY WE PROVIDE THIS NOTICE
Financial companies choose how they share your personal information, and federal law, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and its implementing regulations, requires
us to tell you how we collect, share, and protect that information. We are providing this notice so that you understand what we do and the choices you may have. The types
of personal information we collect and share depend on the product or service you have with us and on the nature of our relationship. We furnish this notice when you
become a customer and, as required by applicable law, on a periodic basis for so long as you maintain a continuing relationship with Fairway Independent Mortgage.
When you are no longer our customer, we continue to share your information as described in this notice, subject to the limitations imposed by law. Nothing in this notice is
intended to expand or restrict any rights you may have under other agreements between you and Fairway Independent Mortgage or under applicable federal and state law.
If state law provides you with additional rights or protections, we will honor those rights to the extent they apply to your account and to the information we maintain about
you.
§ 2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT
We collect personal information about you in connection with providing you a financial product or service. The information we collect depends on the product or service
you request and may include your name, address, and telephone number; your Social Security number and date of birth; your income, employment information, and assets;
and your account balances, payment history, and transaction or loss history. We obtain much of this information directly from you on applications and other forms, and
through your communications with us by telephone, in writing, or electronically.
We also collect your personal information from others, such as credit bureaus, affiliates, and other companies. For example, we may receive your credit history,
creditworthiness, and credit scores from consumer reporting agencies, and we may receive verification of employment, deposits, and other account information from third
parties you authorize. We maintain records relating to the origination, servicing, and, where applicable, the transfer of your loan, including documents you sign at closing
and information generated during the life of your account.
The categories of information we collect and the manner in which we use that information may change over time as products, services, and legal requirements evolve.
Where required, we will update this notice to reflect material changes in our information practices. We collect only the personal information reasonably necessary to
provide the products and services you request, to administer your account, and to comply with our legal and regulatory obligations.
§ 3. HOW WE SHARE YOUR INFORMATION
All financial companies need to share customers' personal information to run their everyday business. In the section that follows, we describe the reasons financial
companies can share their customers' personal information, the reasons Fairway Independent Mortgage chooses to share, and whether you can limit that sharing. Whether
you can limit sharing depends on the reason for which the information is shared and on the protections afforded to you by federal and state law.
We may share your personal information for our everyday business purposes, such as to process your transactions, maintain your account, respond to court orders and legal
investigations, or report to credit bureaus. We may also share for our own marketing purposes, to offer our products and services to you, and for joint marketing with other
financial companies with which we have formal agreements. For these reasons, sharing may occur without your ability to limit it, except where federal or state law
provides otherwise.
We may further share your personal information for purposes related to our affiliates' everyday business, including information about your transactions and experiences
and, in certain circumstances, information about your creditworthiness. Sharing for nonaffiliates to market to you, and certain sharing of credit information among
affiliates, are categories of sharing that you may have the right to limit, as more fully described in the sections of this notice addressing affiliates, nonaffiliates, and your
choices.
§ 4. REASONS YOU CANNOT LIMIT SHARING
There are certain everyday business purposes for which federal law does not permit you to limit our sharing of your personal information. These include sharing necessary
to process your transactions, to maintain and service your account, to respond to court orders, subpoenas, and legal investigations, and to report to credit bureaus and other
consumer reporting agencies. We also share as necessary to protect against or prevent actual or potential fraud, unauthorized transactions, claims, or other liability, and to
comply with federal, state, and local laws and regulatory requirements.
In addition, we may disclose your personal information to companies that perform services on our behalf, including providers of document preparation, data processing,
account servicing, mailing, collection, and similar functions. Where we engage such service providers, we require by contract that they maintain the confidentiality and
security of your information and use it only for the limited purposes for which it was disclosed. These disclosures are part of running our everyday business and are not
subject to your right to limit sharing under applicable law.

§ 5. SHARING WITH AFFILIATES
Affiliates are companies related to us by common ownership or control, and they can include both financial and nonfinancial companies. We may share with our affiliates
information about your transactions and experiences with us, such as your account history and payment record. This type of information sharing is permitted by law and is
generally not subject to your right to opt out, although we will honor any additional limitations required under applicable state law.
We may also share among our affiliates certain other information about you, including information we obtain from your application and from consumer reporting agencies
relating to your creditworthiness. Federal law gives you the right to limit the sharing of this category of information so that our affiliates may not use it to market their
products and services to you. If you wish to limit such sharing, you may do so by following the instructions set out in the section of this notice describing your choices and
how to contact us.

instructed otherwise. Your election will remain in effect until you notify us of a change, and we will apply your choice within a reasonable period after we receive and
process your request.
§ 6. SHARING WITH NONAFFILIATES
Nonaffiliates are companies not related to us by common ownership or control, and they can also include both financial and nonfinancial companies. Fairway Independent
Mortgage does not share your personal information with nonaffiliates so that they can market their own products and services to you, except as permitted or required by
law. Where the law would otherwise permit such sharing, we provide you the opportunity to limit it as described in the section of this notice addressing your choices.

connection with the sale, transfer, securitization, or servicing of your loan, and to service providers acting on our behalf. We may also disclose information to nonaffiliates
as necessary to complete or service the transactions you request, to comply with legal process, and to protect our rights and property. Such disclosures are made consistent
with applicable law and the confidentiality safeguards described in this notice.
§ 7. JOINT MARKETING ARRANGEMENTS
A joint marketing arrangement is a formal agreement between nonaffiliated financial companies that together market financial products or services to you. Fairway
Independent Mortgage may share your personal information with other financial companies with which we have such agreements in order to jointly offer financial products
or services that may be of interest to you. We enter into joint marketing arrangements only with companies that agree by contract to use the information solely for the
purposes of the joint marketing and to protect the confidentiality and security of your information.
Federal law does not give you the right to limit sharing for joint marketing of financial products and services, although you may have the right under applicable law to opt
out of receiving certain marketing communications. If you prefer not to receive marketing offers resulting from a joint marketing arrangement, you may contact us using
the information provided at the end of this notice, and we will take reasonable steps to honor your preference within the time permitted by law.
§ 8. YOUR CHOICES AND HOW TO LIMIT SHARING
If you are a new customer, we may begin sharing your information for the purposes described in this notice on the day we send it to you. When you are no longer our
customer, we continue to share your information as described in this notice, subject to applicable law. You may, however, contact us at any time to limit the categories of
sharing for which the law gives you a right to opt out, including the sharing of certain credit information among our affiliates for their marketing purposes and, where
applicable, sharing with nonaffiliates so that they may market to you.

1207158367, and a description of the sharing you wish to limit. Please allow a reasonable processing period of approximately thirty (30) days after we receive your request

unless you wish to change your prior election.

permitted by law. Limiting sharing for marketing purposes will not affect the disclosures we make in order to run our everyday business, to service your account, or to
comply with our legal and regulatory obligations.
§ 9. HOW WE PROTECT YOUR INFORMATION
To protect your personal information from unauthorized access and use, we maintain physical, electronic, and procedural safeguards that comply with applicable federal
and state standards. These measures include computer safeguards, secured files and buildings, access controls, and employee training designed to limit access to your
information to those who need it to provide products and services to you or to otherwise carry out their responsibilities. We periodically review and update our safeguards
as technology and risks change.
We restrict access to your nonpublic personal information to employees, agents, and service providers who have a legitimate business need for it, and we require those
parties to maintain the confidentiality and security of the information. When we engage third parties to perform services on our behalf, we obtain contractual commitments
that they will protect your information and use it only for authorized purposes. Despite these efforts, no system of safeguards can be guaranteed to be completely secure,
and we encourage you to protect your account credentials and to notify us promptly of any suspected unauthorized activity.
§ 10. INFORMATION FOR FORMER CUSTOMERS
Even after you cease to be a customer of Fairway Independent Mortgage, we continue to maintain and protect your personal information in accordance with this notice and

FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE PRIVACY NOTICE — WHAT WE DO WITH
YOUR PERSONAL INFORMATION
Privacy Notice
Issued to Lawrence dIxon

LENDER Fairway Independent Mortgage
LOAN NO. 1207158367
DATE 02/22/2024
BORROWER Lawrence Dixon

This Privacy Notice describes how Fairway Independent Mortgage ("Fairway Independent Mortgage," "we," "us," or "our") collects, shares, and protects the
personal information of consumers and customers in connection with the loan identified as 1207158367 and the related residential financing secured by the
property located at 33968 Leslie Crescent, Alexandraborough, OK 04324. Financial companies choose how they share your personal information, and federal law
gives consumers the right to limit some but not all sharing. This notice also explains the choices available to you and how you may exercise them. Please read it
carefully and retain a copy with your loan records. The provisions of this notice apply throughout the life of your account and after the relationship ends, except as
otherwise required or permitted by law.
§ 1. WHY WE PROVIDE THIS NOTICE
Financial companies choose how they share your personal information, and federal law, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and its implementing regulations, requires
us to tell you how we collect, share, and protect that information. We are providing this notice so that you understand what we do and the choices you may have. The types
of personal information we collect and share depend on the product or service you have with us and on the nature of our relationship. We furnish this notice when you
become a customer and, as required by applicable law, on a periodic basis for so long as you maintain a continuing relationship with Fairway Independent Mortgage.
When you are no longer our customer, we continue to share your information as described in this notice, subject to the limitations imposed by law. Nothing in this notice is
intended to expand or restrict any rights you may have under other agreements between you and Fairway Independent Mortgage or under applicable federal and state law.
If state law provides you with additional rights or protections, we will honor those rights to the extent they apply to your account and to the information we maintain about
you.
§ 2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT
We collect personal information about you in connection with providing you a financial product or service. The information we collect depends on the product or service
you request and may include your name, address, and telephone number; your Social Security number and date of birth; your income, employment information, and assets;
and your account balances, payment history, and transaction or loss history. We obtain much of this information directly from you on applications and other forms, and
through your communications with us by telephone, in writing, or electronically.
We also collect your personal information from others, such as credit bureaus, affiliates, and other companies. For example, we may receive your credit history,
creditworthiness, and credit scores from consumer reporting agencies, and we may receive verification of employment, deposits, and other account information from third
parties you authorize. We maintain records relating to the origination, servicing, and, where applicable, the transfer of your loan, including documents you sign at closing
and information generated during the life of your account.
The categories of information we collect and the manner in which we use that information may change over time as products, services, and legal requirements evolve.
Where required, we will update this notice to reflect material changes in our information practices. We collect only the personal information reasonably necessary to
provide the products and services you request, to administer your account, and to comply with our legal and regulatory obligations.
§ 3. HOW WE SHARE YOUR INFORMATION
All financial companies need to share customers' personal information to run their everyday business. In the section that follows, we describe the reasons financial
companies can share their customers' personal information, the reasons Fairway Independent Mortgage chooses to share, and whether you can limit that sharing. Whether
you can limit sharing depends on the reason for which the information is shared and on the protections afforded to you by federal and state law.
We may share your personal information for our everyday business purposes, such as to process your transactions, maintain your account, respond to court orders and legal
investigations, or report to credit bureaus. We may also share for our own marketing purposes, to offer our products and services to you, and for joint marketing with other
financial companies with which we have formal agreements. For these reasons, sharing may occur without your ability to limit it, except where federal or state law
provides otherwise.
We may further share your personal information for purposes related to our affiliates' everyday business, including information about your transactions and experiences
and, in certain circumstances, information about your creditworthiness. Sharing for nonaffiliates to market to you, and certain sharing of credit information among
affiliates, are categories of sharing that you may have the right to limit, as more fully described in the sections of this notice addressing affiliates, nonaffiliates, and your
choices.
§ 4. REASONS YOU CANNOT LIMIT SHARING
There are certain everyday business purposes for which federal law does not permit you to limit our sharing of your personal information. These include sharing necessary
to process your transactions, to maintain and service your account, to respond to court orders, subpoenas, and legal investigations, and to report to credit bureaus and other
consumer reporting agencies. We also share as necessary to protect against or prevent actual or potential fraud, unauthorized transactions, claims, or other liability, and to
comply with federal, state, and local laws and regulatory requirements.
In addition, we may disclose your personal information to companies that perform services on our behalf, including providers of document preparation, data processing,
account servicing, mailing, collection, and similar functions. Where we engage such service providers, we require by contract that they maintain the confidentiality and
security of your information and use it only for the limited purposes for which it was disclosed. These disclosures are part of running our everyday business and are not
subject to your right to limit sharing under applicable law.
